task_completion.description: Create the base plate and central hub
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 3D cylindrical base plate with a smaller cylindrical hub protruding from its center is visible in the FreeCAD 3D view. 'Pad' features should be visible in the Tree View.

task_completion.description: Create a D-shaped shaft hole through the center of the hub
task_completion.visual_evidence: A hole with one flat edge (D-shape) is visible going through the center of the hub. A 'Pocket' feature should be visible in the Tree View.
A single rectangular protrusion (blade) is visible on top of the base plate, extending from the central hub to the outer edge of the base plate.

task_completion.description: Use a Polar Pattern to create 6 total blades
task_completion.visual_evidence: Exactly 6 identical rectangular blades are arranged in a circular, evenly spaced pattern around the hub. A 'PolarPattern' feature must be visible in the FreeCAD Tree View.

task_completion.description: Save the file to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: The FreeCAD window title bar displays 'radial_impeller' or a save dialog shows the file being saved to '/home/ga/Documents/FreeCAD/radial_impeller.FCStd'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the FreeCAD GUI to build the model.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking on FreeCAD menus, toolbars, and interacting with the 3D view or Tree View, rather than just running a pre-written script in a terminal.

integrity.description: The agent used the Part Design workbench as instructed.
integrity.visual_evidence: The workbench dropdown menu at the top of the FreeCAD interface shows 'Part Design' is selected.

integrity.description: The model was built progressively using sketches and features.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Tree View shows a genuine history of operations (e.g., Body, Sketches, Pads, Pockets, PolarPattern) rather than an imported solid or a single uneditable feature.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file is /home/ga/Documents/FreeCAD/radial_impeller.FCStd. The design must use the PolarPattern, Pad, and Pocket features from FreeCAD's Part Design workbench. The final model must have exactly 6 blades arranged in a polar pattern. The bounding box dimensions should be approximately 60x60x15mm.
